actionLibraryList.title: Straight-Arm Cross Swings
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand tall with your chest up and core engaged, swinging your arms forward and backward at shoulder height.
2.Alternate which arm crosses on top, and open slightly backward to stretch your chest as you swing.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750400156117.jpg","context":"1.Stand tall, chest up, arms out to sides."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750400159914.jpg","context":"1.Swing both arms front to back at shoulder height."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe steadily.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch in the shoulders.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Relax the shoulders (avoid shrugging) and engage the core to keep the body stable.

actionLibraryList.title: Power Knees
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand tall, engage your core, and slightly bend your knees.
2.Lift your knee to hand height, then bring your foot behind your body, alternating reps.
3.Keep your torso stable and core engaged throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand tall with your core nice and tight and your arms over your head
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Jack up one knee while bringing down your hands to tap the knee as it comes up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage you abdomens to initiate and maintain stable.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Position your hands for a defensive stance.\n2.Shift your weight onto the supporting leg.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750737487980.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750737491750.jpg","context":"1.As you lift your knee, your leg should kick out like a kick."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Maintain a steady breathing.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your back upright and avoid rounding your chest.

actionLibraryList.title: Lateral Bear Crawl
actionLibraryList.context: Start in a four-point position with your knees 5-10cm off the ground, back straight and core engaged. Move one hand and the opposite foot slightly to the side, keeping stable and controlled. Focus on activating your shoulders, core, and legs, with your core driving the movement. Breathe naturally, avoiding hip raise or lower back sag. Keep a neutral pelvis, coordinated movements, and slightly bent elbows to reduce impact.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750574480543.jpg","context":"1.Start in a four-point position with your knees hovering 5-10cm off the ground.\n2.Keep your back flat and engage your core."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750574526375.jpg","context":"1.Move one hand and the opposite foot slightly to the side in coordination."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe evenly.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Shoulders, core, and legs stay engaged, with the core driving the limbs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep a neutral pelvis, straight back, coordinated hand and foot movements, and slightly bent elbows.

actionLibraryList.title: Running In Place
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your torso upright and core engaged as you alternate raising your knees quickly to about 45°, with your toes pointing forward. Swing your arms back and forth in rhythm with the movement, maintaining a clean and sharp motion throughout.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750664005870.jpg","context":"1.Alternate raising your knees quickly to about 45°.\n2.Keep your toes naturally pointing forward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe steadily.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your chest, stiff arm swings, or kicking your legs too high, which could cause your body to lean back.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grip to ground level.
2.Grab the handles with both hands and bring them to an upright position.
3.Lift your chest, keep your back straight, and engage your core for stability.
4.Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
5.Push your hips back to initiate the squat, keeping your back straight.
6.Maintain balance throughout the squat, returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with both handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't arch back when you return to the straight. Don't thrust your hips forward at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Big breath at the start of the movement, engaging your glutes, slowly exhale during the ascent.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate standing movement by squeezing your glutes and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your arms naturally extended.\n2.Hips higher than the knees.\n3.Align your knees with your toes.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233975187.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep the knees straight, but do not lock them.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750234126578.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your back as you return to the upright position. Do not over-extend your lower back at the peak contraction.

actionLibraryList.title: Single-Arm Curtsy Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Hold the handle in one hand, letting it hang naturally at your side, while using the other side to maintain balance.
2.Cross the non-weight-bearing leg behind the supporting leg and lower into a curtsy squat.
3.Lower until the front thigh is nearly parallel to the ground, then control your movement to return to standing.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750241580247.jpg","context":"1.Hold the handle in one hand, letting it hang naturally at your side"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750241589257.jpg","context":"1.Cross the non-weight-bearing leg behind the supporting leg and lower into a squat.\n2.Lower the front leg until the thigh is nearly parallel to the ground."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel activation in the gluteus maximus, gluteus medius, quadriceps, and adductors of the supporting leg.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting the knees cave inward or lean too far forward.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Shoulder Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, engaging the core.
2.Grip the Handles with parallel grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
3.Press both handles overhead simultaneously until the arms are fully extended.
4.At the top, squeeze slightly inward and pause briefly, feeling the shoulder contraction.
5.Slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Anchor the pulleys at floor level with both handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don’t hunch your shoulders or lock your elbows, and keep your forearms vertical to the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath smooth throughout. Exhale as you press upward, inhale as you lower the weight.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your deltoid to press the cable overhead.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Drop your shoulders.
2.Keep your forearms vertical to the ground.
3.Stand tall.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748153352593.jpg"},{"context":"1.Drop your shoulders.
2.Keep your arms straight, but don’t lock your elbows.
3.Keep your back straight.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748153356057.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pressing up, inhale while lowering.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Don't hunch shoulders or lock your elbows, keeping your forearms vertical to the floor.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Overhead Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes turned slightly outward. Engage your core.
2.Grip one handle in each hand and raise both arms straight overhead, locking out your elbows.
3.Inhale to prepare, then hinge at the hips and bend the knees to squat down until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly lower, keeping your arms locked overhead throughout.
4.Exhale and drive through your glutes and legs to extend the hips and knees, returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750238462550.jpg","context":"1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.\n2.Hold a handle in each hand, press them overhead with arms fully extended and locked."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750238466304.jpg","context":"1.Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your knees in line with your toes.\n2.Lower until your thighs are parallel or slightly below parallel to the ground."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid bending your arms or allowing the handles to shift forward or backward.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Calf Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Stand in a neutral position, holding the handle with both hands. Brace your core and keep your body stable.                                                                                                                                                       3.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand in a neutral position, holding the handle with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Rise up onto your toes, and fully squeeze the calf muscle group.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you go up, inhale as you go down.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your shoulders down and hold the handle with both hands.\n2.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934516077.jpg"},{"context":"1.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934520001.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your body balanced.

actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you go up, inhale as you go down.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your shoulders down and hold the handle with both hands.\n2.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934516077.jpg"},{"context":"1.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934520001.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your body balanced.
